Title: Xanthomonas oryzae Orphan Response Regulator EmvR Is Involved in Virulence, Extracellular Polysaccharide Production and Cell Motility

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: pilB deletion did not directly test EPS but caused almost complete loss of twitching motility and greatly increased spreading motility; PilB ATPase activity is repressed by EmvR, linking PilB to motility rather than EPS accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PilB is a type IV pilus motor protein identified as an EmvR interactor by co-IP/LC-MS/MS and validated by bacterial two-hybrid and pull-down assays. 2. Purified PilB has ATPase activity in vitro. 3. EmvR binding reduces PilB ATPase activity by ~40%, indicating PilB is the direct biochemical effector through which EmvR modulates T4P-dependent motility. 4. Deletion of pilB nearly abolishes twitching and strongly increases spreading motility.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-IP coupled LC-MS/MS; bacterial two-hybrid assay; pull-down assay; ATPase assay; gene deletion and complementation; motility phenotyping
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: type IV pilus assembly ATPase; secretion NTPase family
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cell membrane-associated cytoplasmic motor for type IV pilus system
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: in vitro ATPase assay with ATP; motility plate assays
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although PilB is central experimentally, it is not a nutrient metabolism gene per se; it is included because it is a core experimentally interrogated gene linked to EPS-associated phenotype context. The study mainly addresses virulence/motility rather than nutrient metabolism.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of pilB nearly abolishes twitching and greatly alters spreading, indicating strong pleiotropic motility effects

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: EmvR positively affects EPS production; deletion slightly reduces EPS whereas complementation/overexpression significantly increases EPS
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. emvR deletion weakens spreading motility, enhances twitching motility, and reduces virulence after spray inoculation. 2. emvR overexpression increases EPS production and spreading but reduces twitching. 3. EmvR physically interacts with PilB and ColS_XOCgx_4036. 4. EmvR represses PilB ATPase activity and functions downstream of ColS_XOCgx_4036. 5. Asp15, Asp59, and Thr87 are essential for EmvR function, and Asp59 is required for phosphorylation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: marker-free deletion; complementation; overexpression; RT-qPCR; virulence assay; co-IP/LC-MS/MS; bacterial two-hybrid; pull-down assay; ATPase assay; site-directed mutagenesis; Phos-tag phosphorylation assay
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: single-domain response regulator (SD-RR) with REC domain
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: tested in cultured bacterial cells; overexpression increased ~8-fold by RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: spray versus infiltration inoculation; in vitro acetyl-phosphate phosphorylation assay
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: EPS contributes to bacterial virulence and surface-associated growth
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper is not a plant nutrient-metabolism study. EPS is a bacterial extracellular polysaccharide linked to pathogenicity, not crop nutritional quality. EmvR is best classified as a common gene here because it is an orphan response regulator lacking a canonical transcription factor output domain and acts mainly through protein-protein interaction.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion reduces virulence under spray inoculation; overexpression alters motility and elevates EPS, implying pleiotropic effects on host interaction
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for disease control by weakening early stomatal invasion and EPS-associated virulence, but not applicable to nutrient trait engineering

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation downstream of sensor histidine kinase
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: extracellular polysaccharide production and motility-associated surface polysaccharide/virulence regulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: EPS contributes to bacterial virulence and surface-associated behavior
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively regulates EPS production and spreading motility and negatively regulates twitching motility
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of colR_XOCgx_4037 reduces EPS production by 27.7%. 2. The mutant shows weakened spreading and enhanced twitching motility. 3. Complementation restores wild-type phenotypes. 4. Overexpression of emvR in the colR mutant restores EPS and spreading, placing EmvR functionally downstream/parallel in the pathway output.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene deletion; complementation; cross-complementation with emvR; EPS quantification; motility assays
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: positive regulator of EPS output in this ColS/ColR branch
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: likely phosphorylated by ColS_XOCgx_4036 upon unknown environmental cue
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native locus for deletion/complementation; plasmid pXUK for complementation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: motility and EPS assays under plate/culture conditions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: mutation impairs spreading and enhances twitching, indicating pleiotropic behavior effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct transcriptional targets were not identified. Study emphasizes divergent EPS regulation relative to ColS, since colS deletion did not reduce EPS.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antibacterial target for reducing virulence-associated EPS, but not relevant for nutrient breeding

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: signal sensing and phosphotransfer to response regulators
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: motility-associated control with indirect connection to EPS-associated virulence phenotypes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: modulates spreading and twitching motility but does not significantly alter EPS production under tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ColS_XOCgx_4036 physically interacts with EmvR. 2. Deletion of colS_XOCgx_4036 weakens spreading and enhances twitching motility. 3. Unlike colR deletion, colS deletion does not significantly affect EPS production. 4. Overexpression of emvR restores colS mutant phenotypes, supporting that EmvR functions downstream of ColS in a branched one-to-two TCS model.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene deletion; complementation; cross-complementation with emvR; bacterial two-hybrid; pull-down assay; phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: upstream signaling node proposed to branch toward ColR and EmvR outputs
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: unknown environmental cue or emergency stimulus
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native locus for deletion/complementation; plasmid pXUK for complementation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: part of branched two-component signaling system with ColR and EmvR
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: motility and EPS assays under plate/culture conditions
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phosphotransfer from ColS to EmvR was proposed but not directly detected by Phos-tag. The paper focuses on pathogenesis and motility, not nutrient metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for disease control by impairing motility-related invasion, not for nutrient engineering
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: co-IP coupled LC-MS/MS used to identify EmvR interactors; no accession reported
